name: ETH 4h Deep Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: continuation is entered only after a deeper pullback resets the move and value is reclaimed, rather than on shallow ema-touch logic
thesis: Cycle 71 wants trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow EMA-touch logic. ETH 4h is the best fit because its 100% chop state makes shallow continuation logic suspect, but deeper resets back into reclaimed value can still create tradable directional legs without requiring rare trend conditions. The edge is the reset-then-go sequence, not momentum chasing.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h deeper resets still fail to produce follow-through after reclaiming value, or if the structure remains too flat to generate enough moves after costs, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back deeply enough to reset positioning, reclaims value and short-term structure, then continues away from the reset zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Deeper resets can clean out weak positioning in mixed/choppy markets. Requiring reclaimed value should improve entry location compared with shallow continuation logic that kept bleeding in prior families.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because it is the clearest chop lane where shallow continuation was already disproven, making a deeper reset interpretation the meaningful structural change.

indicator_role_map.ADX_14: participation floor to avoid dead rebounds

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may be so rotational that reclaimed resets only bounce briefly before folding back into balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but continuation legs are too small, keep the reset trigger and shift to faster first-profit extraction plus tighter runner logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Enough reset entries to clear screen density, with many first-target captures and fewer extended follow-through wins.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h produces credible density and better post-cost behavior than prior shallow continuation families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reset entries that fail before reaching first target.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: lower than prior continuation families because the trigger is reset plus reclaim, not breakout persistence theater

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit lane for proving that deeper reset continuation is meaningfully different from dead shallow pullback logic.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows non-sparse reset-reclaim continuations with positive first-target behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h remains too flat and every reset continuation fails quickly.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate count with many modest continuation legs after reset.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset idea survives on the cycle primary asset with slightly more movement.
test_lanes.continue_if: SOL 4h preserves the same reset-reclaim behavior with better amplitude.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: SOL 4h turns it into false restarts rather than genuine reset continuations.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger reset continuations than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether adjacent timeframe adds density without losing the reset mechanism.
test_lanes.continue_if: SOL 1h increases count while keeping acceptable follow-through.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: SOL 1h becomes noisy shallow pullback behavior again.
